landing.hero_description: A research-grade multi-agent AI system that autonomously optimises academic resource allocation, forecasts demand, resolves conflicts, and learns from administrator feedback.
landing.feature_agents_desc: ARIA deploys specialised agents — ORACLE for forecasting, ARBITER for conflict resolution, HERMES for dialogue, and ADAPT for preference learning.
landing.feature_rlhf_desc: The system continuously learns from administrator decisions, refining its preference model to align recommendations with institutional values.
landing.feature_rag_desc: Every proposal is grounded in retrieved institutional policy documents, historical data, and peer institution benchmarks.
landing.feature_provenance_desc: Full audit trails for every AI-generated recommendation — trace any decision back to its originating data and agent reasoning.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: System overview and live agent activity
dashboard.agent_timeline_subtitle: Live orchestration cycle — hover events for JSON snapshot
dashboard.memory_subtitle: Similar past decisions retrieved by HERMES (RAG)
dashboard.counterfactual_subtitle: What would happen if we chose the alternative proposal?

chat.title: Hermes Chat
chat.subtitle: Conversational interface with the HERMES agent
chat.disclaimer: Final decision rests with the administrator.
chat.restricted_message: This response has been flagged by guardrails. Please rephrase your query.

audit.title: Decision Provenance
audit.subtitle: Interactive audit graph of the ARIA decision chain
audit.zoom_tip: Scroll to zoom • Drag to pan

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Personalise your ARIA experience
settings.model_card_desc: ARIA uses Prophet for forecasting, PPO-based RLHF for preference learning, and RAG with institutional policy documents.
settings.ethics_notice: ARIA is designed with human-in-the-loop principles. All high-impact decisions require administrator approval. No autonomous resource commitment is made without explicit consent.

system.title: System Overview
system.subtitle: ARIA multi-agent architecture and knowledge infrastructure
system.coordinator_desc: Orchestrates agent lifecycle, routes tasks, and manages inter-agent communication
system.knowledge_graph_desc: Entity-relationship map of all institutional resources, policies, and constraints
system.vector_db_desc: Institutional Memory — stores embeddings of decisions, outcomes, and policies for RAG retrieval
system.rlhf_loop_desc: Preference signals from administrator decisions continuously retrain the reward model
system.pipeline_desc: Requests flow from Sentinel detection → Oracle forecasting → Arbiter resolution → Hermes dialogue → Admin approval → Adapt learning

evaluation.title: Evaluation & Metrics
evaluation.subtitle: Research-grade KPI tracking for grant reviewers and accreditation boards
evaluation.mape_desc: Mean Absolute Percentage Error of Oracle's demand forecasts vs actuals
evaluation.conflict_rate_desc: Percentage of resource conflicts resolved by ARBITER without human escalation
evaluation.acceptance_desc: Proportion of ARIA proposals accepted by the administrator
evaluation.rlhf_desc: How well the reward model aligns with administrator preference patterns
evaluation.gini_desc: Gini coefficient of resource utilisation across departments — lower is better
evaluation.audit_desc: Percentage of decisions with complete provenance trails in the knowledge graph

governance.title: Governance & Ethics
governance.subtitle: Privacy-by-design, institutional compliance, and AI risk framework
governance.privacy_pii_desc: ARIA processes only anonymised enrollment statistics. No personally identifiable student data is stored or processed.
governance.privacy_faculty_desc: Faculty availability and workload data is aggregated at department level before processing.
governance.privacy_hitl_desc: Every resource commitment requires explicit administrator approval. ARIA recommends — humans decide.
governance.privacy_guardrail_desc: HERMES operates behind a content guardrail layer that filters policy-violating, discriminatory, or hallucinated outputs.
governance.privacy_rlhf_desc: Administrator feedback signals used for RLHF are collected with explicit informed consent and can be withdrawn.
governance.privacy_provenance_desc: Every AI-generated recommendation is logged with its originating data sources, agent reasoning chain, and decision timestamp.
governance.risk_desc: ARIA is a decision-support system. Forecasts carry inherent uncertainty. Human judgment supersedes all AI recommendations. Risk of algorithmic bias is mitigated through diverse training data and ongoing bias audits.
governance.bias_desc: ARIA's preference learning may reflect historical allocation patterns. The system flags anomalies and provides counterfactual analysis to surface potential bias in recommendations.
governance.compliance_desc: ARIA is designed in alignment with UGC guidelines, AICTE norms, and IEEE/ACM ethical AI principles. Audit logs are exportable for NAAC accreditation reviews.

common.oversight_notice: ARIA operates under human oversight. No decisions are auto-executed.
